10	# ADR-024: Power Escalation and Review Routing Policy
11	
12	**Status:** Accepted
13	**Date:** 2026-05-06
14	**Authors:** bead `ddx-5f1eac4f`
15	
16	## Context
17	
18	DDx now has three overlapping concerns in the bead execution path:
19	
20	- retrying a bead attempt when stronger reasoning could plausibly help
21	- running an adversarial pre-close reviewer gate that is independent from the
22	  implementer
23	- enforcing cost visibility and budget stops without taking ownership of
24	  provider/model routing
25	
26	Fizeau owns harness/provider/model routing. DDx owns bead
27	state, worktree isolation, evidence, gates, review verdicts, cooldowns, and
28	the queue-drain loop. The escalation policy therefore has to be expressed in
29	terms of DDx-owned evidence and abstract power bounds, not concrete model names.
30	
31	Earlier planning used "tier" and profile language. That vocabulary is now
32	migration debt when it implies DDx choosing a provider, model, or fallback chain.
33	The stable contract is `MinPower` / `MaxPower` plus opaque passthrough
34	constraints.
35	
36	## Decision
37	
38	DDx power escalation is a DDx retry-policy decision expressed only by changing
39	the next request's `MinPower` and carrying the same request facts forward.
40	DDx does not choose, substitute, downgrade, or fallback concrete harnesses,
41	providers, or models. Fizeau chooses the concrete route.
42	
43	### Primary Attempt Escalation
44	
45	For a primary bead attempt, DDx may schedule a higher-power retry only when all
46	of the following are true:
47	
48	1. a valid attempt started and produced DDx-owned evidence;
49	2. the outcome is classified as plausibly capability-sensitive; and
50	3. no stop condition, hard passthrough constraint, budget cap, cooldown, or
51	   operator-action class has already taken ownership of the result.
52	
53	Eligible capability-sensitive classes include failed post-run checks,
54	implementation-quality failures, review blocks that identify fixable reasoning
55	or design gaps, and no-changes-after-attempt when the model had valid context.
56	
57	Ineligible classes include routing/passthrough conflicts, missing tools,
58	authentication or quota failures, invalid bead metadata, dirty worktree, hard
59	merge/land conflicts that need human judgment, command-not-found setup failures,
60	and any failure where retry would require DDx to inspect or mutate
61	`--harness`, `--provider`, or `--model`.
62	
63	When a higher-power retry is allowed, DDx computes the next `MinPower` floor
64	from Fizeau's catalog power numbers. It may skip catalog power floors that have
65	no viable auto-routable model, but it still sends only a new `MinPower` bound
66	to Fizeau. Fizeau chooses the concrete route and reports actual model,
67	provider, and power.
68	
69	### Passthrough Stickiness
70	
71	Operator-supplied passthrough constraints are sticky across every retry.
72	
73	If the operator supplied `--harness`, `--provider`, or `--model`, DDx forwards
74	the same values unchanged. If those constraints make a later power request
75	unsatisfiable, DDx records a typed terminal classification such as
76	`blocked_by_passthrough_constraint` or `agent_power_unsatisfied` and reports
77	operator action required. DDx must not remove pins, widen pins, substitute a
78	fallback route, call `ResolveRoute` to work around the conflict, or loop over
79	concrete route names.
80	
81	### Infrastructure And Rate-Limit Fallback
82	
83	Infrastructure failures are not capability failures. Provider 5xx responses,
84	network unreachability, command-not-found, authentication failures, quota
85	exhaustion, and analogous transport/setup failures do not consume escalation
86	budget. DDx records the failure and either leaves the bead immediately
87	reclaimable or applies a retry-after cooldown only when time passing could
88	plausibly fix the same attempt class.
89	
90	HTTP 429 / rate-limit handling is internal to one attempt. The claim stays held,
91	DDx honors a parseable `Retry-After` when present, otherwise uses bounded
92	exponential backoff, and emits `rate-limit-retry` evidence. When the retry
93	budget is exhausted, the attempt falls back to the ordinary `execution_failed`
94	mapping.
95	
96	### Default Adversarial Pre-Close Review Gate
97	
98	Every close-eligible implementation result passes through an adversarial
99	pre-close review gate before DDx mutates the bead to `closed`. Review is enabled
100	by default. Disabling it is an explicit operator override and must record an
101	auditable reason.
102	
103	The gate runs after the implementation attempt has produced stable evidence
104	(`base_rev`, `result_rev`, diff, verification command output, bead metadata, and
105	governing artifacts), and before the durable close mutation. The candidate
106	commit may already exist in git so the reviewer can inspect a stable
107	`result_rev`; the bead is not closed until the review gate approves that result.
108	
109	The default gate dispatches reviewer invocations per the candidate-cycle sequence
110	defined in FEAT-010. Each reviewer runs as a **read-only tool reviewer** in the
111	still-live attempt worktree: the bounded evidence bundle (candidate diff,
112	acceptance criteria, governing artifacts) is the canonical review input; same-worktree
113	read-only tool access (file reads, searches) is supplemental and does not override
114	the bounded bundle. The reviewer cannot write files or create commits. Each reviewer
115	must return a structured verdict with per-acceptance-criterion evidence.
116	
117	**Transitional single-slot allowance.** Until the two-slot quorum reviewer
118	implementation lands, a single reviewer slot satisfies the gate. The removal
119	condition is the landing of the bead that implements two-slot quorum aggregation.
120	
121	DDx requests a reviewer that is stronger than the implementer by using the
122	implementer's actual power as evidence and setting reviewer `MinPower` to a
123	higher floor. DDx also supplies structured correlation facts:
124	
125	- `role=reviewer`
126	- `bead_id`
127	- `attempt_id`
128	- `session_id`
129	- `result_rev`
130	- `review_group_id`
131	- `reviewer_index`
132	- implementer harness/provider/model/power when known
133	
134	These fields are Day-1 observability. DDx records them so operators and metrics
135	can see whether review pairing degraded. Future agent-side routing
136	intelligence may use `Role` and correlation internally, but DDx does not
137	specify that algorithm and must not depend on it.
138	
139	Different-provider review is best effort. If the agent returns a reviewer on
140	the same provider as the implementer, DDx emits a
141	`review-pairing-degraded` event. That event affects triage bias and operator
142	visibility; it is not by itself a review failure.
143	
144	### Review Outcomes And Retry
145	
146	Reviewer output must satisfy the structured verdict contract. A valid approval
147	requires per-AC evidence; an `APPROVE` without evidence is malformed, not a
148	pass. The aggregate gate is conservative:
149	
150	- unanimous reviewer `APPROVE` with per-AC evidence permits close;
151	- any evidenced `REQUEST_CHANGES` or `BLOCK` prevents close;
152	- reviewer disagreement is preserved in the review group evidence, and the
153	  non-approve verdict wins when it cites evidence;
154	- malformed, empty, context-overflow, and transport failures are
155	  `review-error` classes scoped to the reviewed `result_rev` and reviewer.
156	
157	Non-approve reviewer findings are classified before the next action:
158	
159	- `review_fixable_gap` — implementation or test work is missing, incomplete, or
160	  erroneous. DDx schedules a repair cycle on the same bead when `repair_max_cycles`
161	  allows. The next implementation prompt includes the review findings as required
162	  repair context. DDx may raise `MinPower` only when the finding is plausibly
163	  capability-sensitive.
164	- `review_spec_gap` / `review_missing_acceptance` — the bead or governing spec
165	  is ambiguous, unverifiable, contradictory, or missing acceptance criteria. DDx
166	  parks the bead in the `needs_human` operator-attention lane, clears the
167	  active claim, and does not ask another implementer to guess. The operator
168	  resolves that lane by retry, split, obsolete, or defer actions recorded in
169	  the bead history.
170	- `review_too_large` — the result or bead is too broad for bounded review. DDx
171	  runs the intake/decomposition path, blocks or decomposes the parent, and does
172	  not re-run the same monolithic implementation attempt.
173	- `review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope` — the implementation changed forbidden scope,
174	  removed checks, weakened behavior, or otherwise needs explicit repair. If the
175	  repair is mechanical it follows `review_fixable_gap`; otherwise it parks in
176	  `needs_human`.
177	
178	Review errors retry the review path up to `review_max_retries` for the same
179	`result_rev` and reviewer slot. On exhaustion, DDx emits
180	`review-manual-required`, clears the active claim, parks the bead in
181	`needs_human`, and leaves it open for operator review without closing it. This
182	terminal lane resolution is the same contract used by `review_spec_gap` and
183	`review_missing_acceptance`: the bead remains open, carries the human-review
184	context, and waits for an operator decision rather than another automatic
185	implementation attempt. A new implementation result starts a new review-error
186	retry scope, while `review_fixable_gap` continues through the normal repair
187	retry path instead of entering the operator lane.
188	
189	Each implementation/review cycle is append-only. A repair attempt creates a new
190	cycle record linked to the prior review group (`repair_context_from_review_group`)
191	and records its own `base_rev`, `result_rev`, implementer run ids, verification
192	output, reviewer run ids, aggregate verdict, retry/decomposition/block decision,
193	and cost summary. No cycle overwrites prior evidence.
194	
195	### Cost Accounting
196	
197	Primary attempts and review attempts both report usage and cost into the same
198	run evidence stream. FEAT-014 defines the normalized cost signal fields.
199	FEAT-010 owns the queue-drain budget stop. Subscription-bundled and local
200	providers may be excluded from billed-cost caps by explicit cost-class metadata;
201	unknown cost class counts by default.
202	
203	Reviewer cost is not special. When a review invocation reports billable cost,
204	that cost contributes to the same drain-level cap as implementation attempts.
205	If the cap is reached, DDx stops claiming more work and records an observable
206	budget stop instead of disguising the stop as model failure.
207	
208	## Consequences
209	
210	- FEAT-006 owns request construction: `MinPower` / `MaxPower`, passthrough
211	  envelope, role/correlation metadata, and actual power recording.
212	- FEAT-010 owns intake, retry scheduling, stop-condition evaluation,
213	  no-progress handling, adversarial review aggregation, review retry scopes, and
214	  budget stops.
215	- FEAT-014 owns normalized usage, cost, cost-class, and freshness semantics.
216	- FEAT-022 and TD-033 own review evidence assembly and no-tool reviewer mode.
217	- DDx routing lint should treat concrete route mutation in `run`, `try`, or
218	  `work` policy as a regression.
219	
220	## Non-Goals
221	
222	- Specifying Fizeau's internal routing, fallback, provider-health, or model
223	  scoring algorithm.
224	- Guaranteeing a different reviewer provider or model. DDx can request stronger
225	  review, dispatch independent reviewer slots, and record degradation, not force
226	  a route.
227	- Retrying by concrete model name or profile mutation.
228	- Adding a fourth run layer or a review-specific execution substrate.
229

196	or `git rebase` against the prior candidate is permitted in a repair cycle.
197	
198	### Cost Accounting
199	
200	Primary attempts and review attempts both report usage and cost into the same
201	run evidence stream. FEAT-014 defines the normalized cost signal fields.
202	FEAT-010 owns the queue-drain budget stop. Subscription-bundled and local
203	providers may be excluded from billed-cost caps by explicit cost-class metadata;
204	unknown cost class counts by default.
205	
206	Reviewer cost is not special. When a review invocation reports billable cost,
207	that cost contributes to the same drain-level cap as implementation attempts.
208	If the cap is reached, DDx stops claiming more work and records an observable
209	budget stop instead of disguising the stop as model failure.
210

212	
213	- FEAT-006 owns request construction: `MinPower` / `MaxPower`, passthrough
214	  envelope, role/correlation metadata, and actual power recording.

96	### Default Adversarial Pre-Close Review Gate
97	
98	Every close-eligible implementation result passes through an adversarial
99	pre-close review gate before DDx mutates the bead to `closed`. Review is enabled
100	by default. Disabling it is an explicit operator override and must record an
101	auditable reason.
102	
103	The gate runs after the implementation attempt has produced stable evidence
104	(`base_rev`, `result_rev`, diff, verification command output, bead metadata, and
105	governing artifacts), and before the durable close mutation. The candidate
106	commit may already exist in git so the reviewer can inspect a stable
107	`result_rev`; the bead is not closed until the review gate approves that result.
108	
109	The default gate dispatches two independent reviewer invocations. Each reviewer
110	runs in TD-033 no-tool reviewer mode over the same bounded evidence bundle and
111	must return a structured verdict with per-acceptance-criterion evidence.
112	
113	DDx requests a reviewer that is stronger than the implementer by using the
114	implementer's actual power as evidence and setting reviewer `MinPower` to a
115	higher floor. DDx also supplies structured correlation facts:
116	
117	- `role=reviewer`
118	- `bead_id`
119	- `attempt_id`
120	- `session_id`
121	- `result_rev`
122	- `review_group_id`
123	- `reviewer_index`
124	- implementer harness/provider/model/power when known
125	
126	These fields are Day-1 observability. DDx records them so operators and metrics
127	can see whether review pairing degraded. Future agent-side routing
128	intelligence may use `Role` and correlation internally, but DDx does not
129	specify that algorithm and must not depend on it.
130	
131	Different-provider review is best effort. If the agent returns a reviewer on
132	the same provider as the implementer, DDx emits a
133	`review-pairing-degraded` event. That event affects triage bias and operator
134	visibility; it is not by itself a review failure.

136	### Review Outcomes And Retry
137	
138	Reviewer output must satisfy the structured verdict contract. A valid approval
139	requires per-AC evidence; an `APPROVE` without evidence is malformed, not a
140	pass. The aggregate gate is conservative:
141	
142	- unanimous reviewer `APPROVE` with per-AC evidence permits close;
143	- any evidenced `REQUEST_CHANGES` or `BLOCK` prevents close;
144	- reviewer disagreement is preserved in the review group evidence, and the
145	  non-approve verdict wins when it cites evidence;
146	- malformed, empty, context-overflow, and transport failures are
147	  `review-error` classes scoped to the reviewed `result_rev` and reviewer.
148	
149	Non-approve reviewer findings are classified before the next action:
150	
151	- `review_fixable_gap` — implementation or test work is missing, incomplete, or
152	  erroneous. DDx schedules a new implementation cycle on the same bead when
153	  retry budgets allow. The next implementation prompt includes the review
154	  findings as required repair context. DDx may raise `MinPower` only when the
155	  finding is plausibly capability-sensitive.
156	- `review_spec_gap` / `review_missing_acceptance` — the bead or governing spec

158	  parks the bead in the `needs_human` operator-attention lane, clears the

### Escalation, fallback, retry, and review decision tree
input.new_string:
The prior cycle remains immutable so operators can answer what changed, why the
first result was rejected, and why the final result passed.

### Candidate-Cycle Pipeline

A **candidate** is the stable commit produced by one implementation pass and
pinned as the subject of the review gate before land. A candidate exists when
the implementation produces at least one commit and post-run checks have been
attempted.

A **candidate ref** is a project-root git ref
(`refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>`) that pins the candidate
commit so reviewers can inspect a stable revision even after worktree cleanup.

A **cycle** is one implementation pass plus its associated review gate: one
implementation pass that produces a candidate, the read-only tool reviewer gate
run against that candidate in the still-live worktree, and the resulting
disposition (land, repair, or stop).

A **repair cycle** is a cycle triggered when the prior cycle's review verdict is
`review_fixable_gap`. The repair implementation runs in the same still-live
attempt worktree and is **append-only** — no `git reset`, `git commit --amend`,
`git squash`, or `git rebase` against the prior candidate is permitted. The
repair prompt includes the prior review group's findings as required context.

A **read-only tool reviewer** is a reviewer agent that runs in the still-live
attempt worktree with access to read-only filesystem tools (file reads,
searches) but no write tools. The bounded evidence bundle assembled per FEAT-022
is the canonical review input; same-worktree read-only tool access is
supplemental and does not override the bounded bundle. The reviewer cannot
create commits or modify worktree state.

**Terminal dispositions** for a candidate-cycle attempt:
- `landed` — candidate was approved and merged to the base branch.
- `preserved` — candidate is approved but land was deferred; worktree retained.
- `parked` — repair budget exhausted without approval; bead moved to `needs_human`.
- `conflicted` — approved candidate failed to land due to merge conflict; bead
  returned to `open` for re-attempt with a fresh `base_rev`.
- `budget-stopped` — drain-level cost or no-progress budget tripped; worktree
  preserved with evidence.

**Candidate-cycle sequence** (in the still-live attempt worktree, before land):

1. Create isolated worktree from `base_rev`.
2. Run implementation pass; collect `base_rev`, `result_rev`, run ids,
   verification output, and cost facts.
3. Pin candidate ref; emit `candidate-pinned`.
4. Run candidate checks (post-run tests, lint, gates). Failure emits
   `candidate-checks-failed`; plausibly capability-sensitive failures are
   eligible for a repair cycle when `repair_max_cycles` allows.
5. Dispatch read-only tool reviewers in the still-live worktree, **before land**.
   Record `review_group_id`, reviewer run ids, per-AC verdict, and cost.
6. On unanimous `APPROVE`: land, emit `final-result-landed`, close bead, clean up.
7. On `REQUEST_CHANGES` / `BLOCK` classified `review_fixable_gap` within
   `repair_max_cycles`: emit `repair-cycle-started`, run append-only repair in
   same worktree, return to step 3.
8. On `repair_max_cycles` exhausted: emit `repair-cycle-exhausted`, preserve
   worktree, park bead in `needs_human`.
9. On approved candidate that fails to land (merge conflict): emit
   `approved-land-conflict`, release claim, return bead to `open`.

**Budget axes** (each is distinct from cost, rate-limit/provider, and
no-progress budgets):

- **`repair_max_cycles`** — maximum repair cycles per attempt. Exhaustion fires
  `repair-cycle-exhausted` and parks the bead in `needs_human`; it does not
  consume the no-progress counter.
- **`review_max_retries_per_candidate`** — maximum reviewer retry attempts for a
  single candidate ref when reviewer invocations fail. Exhaustion fires
  `review-manual-required`. A new candidate ref (next repair cycle or fresh
  `ddx try`) resets this counter independently; it is strictly per-candidate.

These axes do not interact with the drain-level cost cap (FEAT-014), the
rate-limit retry budget internal to a single attempt, or the no-progress counter
tracking consecutive attempts with no commit.

**Transitional single-slot allowance.** Until the two-slot quorum reviewer bead
lands, a single reviewer slot satisfies the review gate. The removal condition
is the landing of the bead that implements two-slot quorum aggregation.

### Escalation, fallback, retry, and review decision tree

- `triage-ambiguous` — readiness could not safely clarify the bead
- `auto-triage` — TriageContract: triage path mutated labels/status

Candidate-cycle events (FEAT-010 candidate-cycle pipeline):

- `candidate-pinned` — layer 2 pinned a candidate ref for the current cycle's
  `result_rev` before dispatching reviewers; body carries `candidate_ref`,
  `cycle_index`, and `attempt_id`
- `candidate-checks-failed` — post-run verification (tests, lint, gates) failed
  against the candidate; body carries check command and exit status
- `repair-cycle-started` — a repair cycle has been initiated in the still-live
  worktree; body carries `cycle_index`, `repair_context_from_review_group`, and
  `attempt_id`
- `repair-cycle-exhausted` — `repair_max_cycles` was reached for this attempt;
  bead moves to the `needs_human` lane
- `approved-land-conflict` — the candidate received unanimous `APPROVE` but merge
  to the base branch conflicted; bead is re-queued at its base revision without
  blame on the implementer or reviewer
- `final-result-landed` — the approved candidate has been merged to the base
  branch; immediately precedes the `closed-merged` lifecycle event

data.text:
All ACs verified. Commit `0b8979c4d` lands the spec for the candidate-cycle review and repair pipeline:

- **FEAT-010**: Added `### Candidate-Cycle Pipeline` section defining all vocabulary (candidate, candidate ref, cycle, repair cycle, read-only tool reviewer, terminal dispositions), the 9-step pre-land sequence, distinct budget axes (`repair_max_cycles`, `review_max_retries_per_candidate`), and transitional single-slot allowance. Updated decision-tree steps 4/5 to reference the pipeline and use the named budget axes.
- **ADR-024**: Selected read-only tool reviewer as the capability model; stated review runs pre-land in still-live worktree; added append-only repair constraint (no reset/amend/squash/rebase); renamed `review_max_retries` → `review_max_retries_per_candidate`; added transitional single-slot allowance; updated Consequences from "no-tool reviewer" → "read-only tool reviewer".
- **TD-031**: Added 7 candidate-cycle events to §4 (`candidate-pinned`, `candidate-checks-failed`, `review-request-changes`, `repair-cycle-started`, `repair-cycle-exhausted`, `approved-land-conflict`, `final-result-landed`).
- **FEAT-022**: Added §2a stating the bounded evidence bundle is canonical and same-worktree access is supplemental/read-only.
- **FEAT-016**: Fixed pre-existing `[[MET-002]]` wiki-link that caused a circular dependency cycle in the doc graph; `ddx doc audit` now exits 0.
